title: composed-annotator
description: Annotation extension for the composed layer. Documents the two response-contingent US-modulation annotations — @omission and @avoidance — that express the execution-time rules by which a recorded response alters scheduled US delivery for a given trial. Attaches to respondent primitives hosted inside experiment-layer phase blocks (CER, PIT, autoshape, omission, two-process-theory).

omission.description: Response-contingent US cancellation rule for negative-automaintenance / omission procedures (Williams & Williams, 1969). When the specified response class is emitted during the specified temporal window, the scheduled US is cancelled for that trial; the Pavlovian CS-US pairing otherwise proceeds unchanged.

response.description: Identifier of the observable response class whose emission cancels the scheduled US on that trial.

during.description: Temporal window in which the cancellation rule applies. 'cs' (default) limits cancellation to responses emitted while the CS is on. 'iti' applies only during the inter-trial interval. 'trial' spans the full trial including both windows.

errors.OMISSION_RESPONSE_REQUIRED: response is required and must be a non-empty string
errors.OMISSION_DURING_UNKNOWN: during must be one of {cs, iti, trial} when provided
errors.OMISSION_UNKNOWN_PARAM: only response and during are admissible parameters

omission.reference: Williams, D. R., & Williams, H. (1969). Auto-maintenance in the pigeon: Sustained pecking despite contingent non-reinforcement. Journal of the Experimental Analysis of Behavior, 12(4), 511-520. https://doi.org/10.1901/jeab.1969.12-511

avoidance.description: Response-contingent avoidance rule for discriminated-avoidance procedures (Solomon & Wynne, 1954). When the specified response class is emitted during the CS, the scheduled US is cancelled (mode=cancel) or the CS offset / US onset is postponed (mode=postpone).

response.description: Identifier of the observable response class that prevents the US on a given trial.

mode.description: Avoidance modality. 'cancel' (default): the response during CS cancels the scheduled US for that trial. 'postpone': the response postpones CS offset / US onset (e.g. Sidman-style free-operant avoidance on a discriminated schedule).

errors.AVOIDANCE_RESPONSE_REQUIRED: response is required and must be a non-empty string
errors.AVOIDANCE_MODE_UNKNOWN: mode must be one of {cancel, postpone} when provided
errors.AVOIDANCE_UNKNOWN_PARAM: only response and mode are admissible parameters

avoidance.reference: Solomon, R. L., & Wynne, L. C. (1954). Traumatic avoidance learning: The principles of anxiety conservation and partial irreversibility. Psychological Review, 61(6), 353-385. https://doi.org/10.1037/h0054540

composition_rules.attachment: Both @omission and @avoidance attach at schedule level (postfix on a respondent primitive) and are parsed into the AnnotatedSchedule node enclosing the primitive. Attaching them elsewhere is not an error at parse time but has no execution-time effect.
composition_rules.disjointness: At most one of @omission or @avoidance may be attached to a single respondent primitive; their execution-time semantics conflict (omission cancels, avoidance cancels-or-postpones). Programs that need both simultaneously must decompose into separate phases.
